Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Results explicitly identify FtSAGH1 as FtPinG0707407000.01.

Source_Species.reason: The study is on Tartary buckwheat and FtSAGH1 is a buckwheat gene.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper title and context indicate Tartary buckwheat, but the exact Latin name 'Fagopyrum tataricum' does not appear in the provided text.

Applied_Species.reason: FtSAGH1 was tested in Tartary buckwheat hairy roots and ectopically in Arabidopsis.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis is named, but the exact Latin binomials 'Fagopyrum tataricum; Arabidopsis thaliana' are not explicitly written in the provided text.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Hairy roots used Tartary buckwheat cv. Pinku; Arabidopsis controls are described as Col-0.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: SA/salicylic acid is the metabolite associated with FtSAGH1 and is produced from SAG by the enzyme.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Figure 2b labels SA among phenolic acids.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Results state SA induces systemic acquired resistance and is indispensable in plant defence.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results state FtSAGH1 expression is prominent in stem, leaf and mature seed; this is the only site-related statement tied to the SA/FtSAGH1 module.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The text describes FtSAGH1 as releasing active salicylic acid from SAG, fitting SA glycoside hydrolysis/homeostasis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: In vitro and overexpression data specifically support SAG-to-SA de-glycosylation.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The gene is explicitly named Salicylic Acid Glycoside Hydrolase 1 (FtSAGH1).

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: FtPinG0707407000.01 is explicitly described as encoding a glycoside hydrolase.

Primary_Substrate.reason: In vitro assay tested salicylic acid 2-O-β-glucoside (SAG) as substrate.

Primary_Product.reason: The enzyme assay and hairy-root overexpression show release/increase of SA.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Results explicitly state FtSAGH1 hydrolyzes SAG to SA.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Because FtSAGH1 converts stored glycoside SAG into free SA, describing it as a salvage/recycling step is directly inferable.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The enzyme directly forms the measured target product SA from SAG.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper supports it as important for active SA release, and the qualifier 'not formally established as rate-limiting' matches the evidence.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Hairy-root overexpression elevated SA and reduced SAG; Arabidopsis overexpression enhanced resistance to Rhizoctonia solani.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point is described in Results: mGWAS+sweep overlap, positive expression-SA correlation, in vitro SAG hydrolysis plus hairy-root SA/SAG shift, and enhanced pathogen resistance in Arabidopsis.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Figure S11/Results state FtSAGH1 expression is prominent in stem, leaf and mature seed.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: For the applied buckwheat system, the same tissue-expression statement is reported.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are explicitly described in Results/Methods.

Omics_Data.reason: The paper used widely targeted LC-MS/MS metabolomics and mGWAS with 1,004,824 SNPs.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods state 35S:FtSAGH1-GFP in pCAMBIA1302 for overexpression.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Results compare domestication groups HW vs SL and perform Rhizoctonia solani AG-4 HGI infection assays.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: General breeding relevance is inferable from conclusions, but specific recommendations for marker-assisted selection or editing are not directly tested in Results.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Discussion notes SA is negatively correlated with grain yield, but no direct FtSAGH1-specific tradeoff is experimentally established.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results explicitly state two nearby glycoside hydrolase genes did not yield detectable SA from SAG under tested conditions.
